## 2026-06-04 - Codex

- Completed T-1210 auto-research skill creation for the user request to turn the Karpathy autoresearch/self-improvement loop into a reusable skill.
- Commit: `aae01277` (`feat(skills): T-1210 add auto-research loop skill`).
- Added `.agents/skills/auto-research/SKILL.md` with a bounded research -> implement -> verify -> A/B compare -> adopt/revert -> record workflow, including GitHub PR/dependency triage and browser/app-click QA gates.
- Added `.agents/skills/auto-research/references/karpathy-autoresearch.md` and `references/loop-contract.md` to separate the source concept, product adaptation, evidence manifest, adoption gate, UI smoke, and GitHub triage rules from the lean skill body.
- Added deterministic helpers: `.agents/skills/auto-research/scripts/ab_decision.py` for weighted baseline/candidate scoring and `.agents/skills/auto-research/scripts/github_project_inventory.py` for local project, workflow, Dependabot, and open PR inventory.
- Fixed `.agents/skills/skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py` to read `SKILL.md` using UTF-8 after default Windows CP949 decoding failed on Korean trigger text in the new skill.
- Verification: `python .agents/skills/skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py .agents/skills/auto-research` passed; `python -m py_compile` passed for both new scripts and the validator; inline `ab_decision.decide(...)` returned `adopt_candidate`; `python .agents/skills/auto-research/scripts/github_project_inventory.py --root . --include-prs` ran and found 7 local project surfaces plus 27 open PRs (18 BLOCKED, 26 Dependabot); scoped `git diff --check -- .agents/skills/auto-research .agents/skills/skill-creator/scripts/quick_validate.py` exited 0 with a CRLF warning only.
- Boundary: existing unrelated `projects/knowledge-dashboard` dirty-tree work was left untouched. `main` is still ahead of origin, so any push would publish pending ahead commits too.

- Fixed current browser findings on the dirty `projects/knowledge-dashboard` product bundle: initial unauthenticated load now calls `/api/auth/session` first and avoids protected data API 401s; `GET /api/auth/session` returns HTTP 200 `{authenticated:false}` for missing sessions; the login form no longer shows the failed-key alert before a user submission; the API-key password input has hidden username/autocomplete context; QA/chart Recharts containers use stable heights/min-widths to prevent tab-switch measurement warnings; and `rate-limit.test.mts` uses `const` for non-reassigned timestamps.

## 2026-06-04 - Codex

- Completed T-1216 pre-push implementation by superseding the useful core of Dependabot PR #95 on current `main`.
- Changed `.github/workflows/full-test-matrix.yml`: frontend-active-apps job now uses `pnpm/action-setup@v6` instead of `pnpm/action-setup@v4`.

## 2026-06-06 - Codex

- Closed T-1389 as a bounded `$auto-research` `hanwoo-dashboard` protected-route callback-origin browser-QA cycle.
- Browser baseline: `http://127.0.0.1:3001/subscription/success` redirected to `http://127.0.0.1:3001/login?callbackUrl=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A3001%2Fsubscription%2Fsuccess`, so the protected route returned to `localhost` instead of the request host.
- Research: official Auth.js docs confirm `callbacks.authorized({ request, auth })` is invoked from middleware and may return a custom `NextResponse`; installed `next-auth` source showed `AUTH_URL`/`NEXTAUTH_URL` rewrites `request.nextUrl` origin before default callback URL construction.
- Changed `projects/hanwoo-dashboard/src/auth.js`: unauthenticated protected routes now return an explicit login `NextResponse.redirect()` and reconstruct callback origin from `x-forwarded-proto`, `x-forwarded-host`, and `host` headers before setting `callbackUrl`.
- Changed `projects/hanwoo-dashboard/src/lib/error-pages-wiring.test.mjs`: added source-contract coverage so the auth proxy cannot regress to `request.nextUrl.origin` or `request.nextUrl.href` callback construction.
- Browser QA: candidate redirect kept `callbackHost=127.0.0.1:3001`, protected-route login redirect still worked, console warnings/errors were `0`, and screenshot artifact is `output/playwright/hanwoo-t1389-callback-origin.png`.
- Verification: focused source test passed `13/13`; full Hanwoo `npm test` passed `508/508`; ESLint passed; Hanwoo project QC passed test/lint/build/smoke; path-limited diff-check passed with CRLF warnings only.
- Code-review gate: `py -3.13 execution\code_review_gate.py --staged --json` returned advisory WARN `risk_score=0.50`, covered by focused/full tests and browser QA. The first `python execution\code_review_gate.py --staged --json` failed only because that interpreter could not import `code_review_graph`.

## 2026-06-06 - Codex

- Closed T-1394 as a bounded `$auto-research` `hanwoo-dashboard` protected-route login fragment browser-QA cycle.
- Browser baseline: `http://127.0.0.1:3001/admin/diagnostics?tab=health#db` reached `http://127.0.0.1:3001/login?callbackUrl=http%3A%2F%2F127.0.0.1%3A3001%2Fadmin%2Fdiagnostics%3Ftab%3Dhealth#db`, so the protected route hash polluted the login URL.
- Research note: RFC 9110 redirect semantics say a 3xx `Location` without a fragment inherits the original request fragment; including an explicit fragment blocks that inheritance.
- Changed `projects/hanwoo-dashboard/src/auth.js`: unauthenticated protected-route redirects now use `${loginUrl.href}#login`, preserving the safe same-origin callback while replacing inherited protected-route hashes with an owned login fragment.
- Changed `projects/hanwoo-dashboard/src/lib/error-pages-wiring.test.mjs`: added source-contract coverage for the `#login` redirect target.
- Browser QA: final URL ended at `http://127.0.0.1:3001/login?callbackUrl=http%3A%2F%2F127.0.0.1%3A3001%2Fadmin%2Fdiagnostics%3Ftab%3Dhealth#login`, console warnings/errors were `0`, and screenshot artifact is `output/playwright/hanwoo-t1394-fragment-cleared.png`.
- Verification: manual header check returned `307` with `Location: /login?...#login`; focused Node tests passed `15/15`; ESLint passed; full Hanwoo `npm.cmd test` passed `510/510`; Hanwoo project QC passed test/lint/build/smoke; path-limited `git diff --check` passed with CRLF warnings only.
- A/B decision: `.agents/skills/auto-research/scripts/ab_decision.py .tmp\ab-manifest-t1394.json --json` returned `adopt_candidate` with `score_delta=1.2727272727272727`.
- Commit closeout: `eec15103 fix(hanwoo): T-1394 clear inherited login fragments` is local only; no push was performed and T-251 was not retried. Preserve unrelated workspace Shorts Analytics WIP outside T-1394.

- External/current research: official Next.js App Router error-handling docs distinguish expected errors from uncaught exceptions; official redirect docs note redirect throws internally, so the candidate preserves redirect/notFound control-flow instead of converting those to data fallbacks.
- Changed `projects/hanwoo-dashboard/src/lib/dashboard/initial-data-fallback.mjs`: added serializable empty fallback shapes for dashboard pages, summary, farm settings, market price, profitability, and normalized degraded-load status.
- Changed `projects/hanwoo-dashboard/src/app/page.js`: replaced the raw initial `Promise.all([...])` with per-section `loadInitialDataSection()` wrappers that catch data-loader failures, preserve Next control-flow errors, and pass `initialDataLoadStatus` into `DashboardClient`.
- Changed `projects/hanwoo-dashboard/src/components/DashboardClient.js`: added a Korean degraded-storage status banner with refresh, normalized `initialDataLoadStatus`, skipped field-mode full-cattle preload when cattle initial data is degraded, and let the home header action row wrap on mobile.
- Changed `projects/hanwoo-dashboard/src/app/globals.css`: made the bottom tab bar an 8-column grid with shrink-safe tab items so all tabs stay inside the mobile viewport.
- Changed tests: added `initial-data-fallback.test.mjs` for fallback shapes/status/source contracts and updated `profitability-copy.test.mjs` for the new `initialData.profitability` prop path.
- Browser QA: candidate authenticated `/` verified `hasErrorBoundary=false`, degraded banner visible, field-mode button visible, `docScrollWidth/bodyScrollWidth=385`, field mode reachable with quick search/back button visible, and no new `/api/dashboard/cattle?limit=100` 500 after field-mode entry. - Changed `workspace/tests/test_youtube_metadata.py`, `workspace/tests/test_youtube_uploader.py`, and `workspace/tests/test_shorts_manager.py`: locked helper limits, uploader upload kwargs, and Shorts Manager fallback metadata behavior.

## 2026-06-08 - Codex

- Completed T-1579 as the fourth LLM Wiki autonomous reinforcement cycle after the initial wiki build.

Local `projects/blind-to-x/config.yaml` is intentionally excluded because it can carry secrets.
- Added `docs/wiki/llm/17-config-facts.md`; updated `docs/wiki/llm/README.md`, `docs/wiki/llm/14-maintenance-verification.md`, `docs/wiki/llm/16-code-facts.md`, `docs/wiki/llm/06-per-project.md`, and `workspace/directives/INDEX.md`; regenerated `docs/wiki/llm/source-inventory.json`, `docs/wiki/llm/code-facts.json`, and `docs/wiki/llm/config-facts.json`.

- Expanded `workspace/tests/test_llm_wiki_audit.py` from 13 to 17 tests, covering safe YAML extraction, missing config manifest, provider-order drift failure, healthy manifest pass, and secret non-leakage.

## 2026-06-08 - Codex

- Completed T-1640 as an auto-research selector dirty-plan freshness fix.
- Reproduced the bug with live artifacts: after regenerating `.tmp/scoped-dirty-worktree-handoff-plan-current.json` to dirty count `83` and signature `cd4247dbc9342513840b311645a67368c8962134a47188af1ffe2f7b5d6c8b7b`, `next_experiment_selector.py --dirty-handoff-plan ...` still returned `status=candidate`, selected `dirty_worktree_handoff`, and reported `adoptable_candidate_count=1`.
- Root cause: `_dirty_handoff_plan_matches_inventory()` treated `freshness.current` as current-plan validity, but `dirty_worktree_handoff_plan.py` uses `freshness` to describe whether the previous plan matched the newly generated plan. A newly generated current plan can show previous-plan `stale` while its own dirty signature exactly matches the current GitHub inventory.
- Fixed `next_experiment_selector.py` to treat exact dirty-count and dirty-path signature matching as the current-plan validity signal, require both plan and inventory paths to be available, and keep `freshness` as evidence only.
- Added `workspace/tests/test_auto_research_next_experiment_selector.py` coverage for a matching dirty handoff signature with `freshness=stale` blocking repeated handoff generation.
- Verification passed focused selector pytest (`20 passed`), related selector/debug-inventory/launch pytest (`71 passed`), Ruff format/check for touched files, `py_compile`, path-limited `git diff --check` with CRLF warnings only, and live selector now returns `status=blocked`, `selected_kind=dirty_worktree_handoff_current`, `adoptable_candidate_count=0`.
- Graph detect risk is `0.00`; full dirty-tree `code_review_gate.py --base HEAD --json` remains advisory WARN (`risk_score=0.55`) due broad pre-existing WIP/untracked graph-relevant files. Removed `.tmp/pytest-t1639-*`; no staging, commit, push, revert, `update_goal`, or Hanwoo T-251 retry was performed.

- Completed T-1713 as a `claude-goal` invoke help non-mutating UX fix in `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`.
- Direct CLI use reproduced that `python goal/scripts/claude_goal.py invoke --help` behaved like a new objective instead of help, printing `Action: set` and storing `Objective: --help` in the active goal DB.
- Cleared the accidental default DB row created during reproduction, then used isolated temp goal DBs for all follow-up reproduction and verification.
- Root cause: `invoke` uses `argparse.REMAINDER`, and the legacy `invoke` dispatch path passed `--help` through to slash-command parsing before argparse help handling, so unknown command text fell through to `set_goal()`.
- Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` with non-mutating `INVOKE_HELP` handling for `invoke -h/--help` in both main and legacy invoke paths.
- Added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_invoke_help_is_non_mutating` to lock that `--help` and `-h` show help, do not print `Action: set`, and leave `status` at no active goal.
- Verification passed direct temp-DB CLI QA in `.tmp/claude-goal-t1713-cli-qa.json`, focused pytest (`1 passed, 94 deselected`), full claude-goal pytest (`136 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, `git diff --check` with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`.
- External refs checked Python argparse help behavior, GitHub CLI subcommand help, and Vercel CLI `--help` global option.
- Removed this loop's temp DB and pytest basetemp dirs. No staging, commit, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or Hanwoo T-251 retry was performed.

## 2026-06-08 - Codex

- Completed T-1716 as a `claude-goal` direct `set` help non-mutating UX fix in `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`.
- Reproduced the bug with a direct CLI help mutation scan and isolated temp goal DBs: `python goal/scripts/claude_goal.py set --help` returned `0` but printed `Action: set` and stored an active goal whose objective was `--help`; `set -h` had the same state-mutation risk.
- Root cause: `_run_legacy_main_command()` intercepts legacy `set` before argparse, and because `set` uses raw/remainder-style objective text, `--help` was treated as objective content instead of being passed to the direct `set` argparse help renderer.
- Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by letting legacy `set -h/--help` fall through to the existing argparse parser.
- Added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_set_help_is_non_mutating` to lock that direct `set --help` and `set -h` show help, do not print `Action: set`, and leave no active goal.

- Removed this loop's temp repro and pytest basetemp directories after path-safety verification. No staging, commit, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or Hanwoo T-251 retry was performed.

## 2026-06-08 - Codex

- Reproduced the bug with a real CLI flow, temp settings, and temp goal DB: a strict healthy install-health JSON artifact whose first `checks` item kept `name=settings_json` and `status=ok` but changed `detail` to `tampered check detail` still passed both direct `doctor --install --verify-json <json>` and slash `invoke` verification with return code `0`.

## 2026-06-08 - Codex

- Completed T-1727 as a `claude-goal` cp949 output UnicodeEncodeError fix in `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`.
- Continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop after T-1726/T-1726b, preserving existing WIP; did not stage, commit, push, revert, call `update_goal`, edit root product code, or retry Hanwoo T-251.
- Reproduced with isolated temp goal state and objective `alpha \u2014 beta`: under cp949 output, `set` exited `1` after mutating state and `status` exited `1` with `UnicodeEncodeError`/`Traceback` from `print(render_invoke_result(...))`.
- Root cause: CLI rendering produced Unicode text successfully, but stdout/stderr used strict cp949 error handling, so unencodable goal text raised while printing output.

## 2026-06-09 - Codex

- Completed T-1742 as a `claude-goal` legacy SQLite schema migration fix in `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`.
- Continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP and the completed T-1741 invoke set fallback preflight surface; did not stage nested code, push, revert, call `update_goal`, edit root product code, or retry Hanwoo T-251.
- Reproduced with isolated legacy SQLite state that a `goals` table missing `active_started_at`/`metadata_json` and an `events` table missing `goal_id`/`detail` made `status`, `json`, `contract`, `pause`, and `doctor` fail with `IndexError: No item with that key` tracebacks.
- Root cause: `init_db()` only used `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`, so existing older SQLite tables were never migrated to the current schema before readers accessed newer columns.
- Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by adding idempotent `goals`/`events` column migrations during DB initialization.
- Added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_init_db_migrates_legacy_goal_schema_without_traceback`.
- Direct after-fix QA in `.tmp/claude-goal-t1742-legacy-schema-migration-qa.json` confirms `status`, `json`, `contract`, `doctor --json`, and `pause` return exit `0`, add the missing columns, preserve empty metadata, record the pause event, and print no traceback.
- Verification passed focused/adjacent pytest (`3 passed`), full isolated pytest with `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`176 passed`), Ruff check, scoped Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, `git diff --check` with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`.
- Known environment notes: pytest without repo-local `--basetemp` failed before tests on Windows temp `PermissionError`; whole-tree Ruff format check failed only because it scanned old `.tmp` pytest copies, while `goal tests` passed.

## 2026-06-09 - Codex

- Completed T-1745b as a `claude-goal` installer backup-directory guard fix in `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`.
- Continued the `/goal` reproduce -> isolate -> root-cause -> fix -> verify loop while preserving existing nested WIP and the completed T-1744 installer malformed Stop entry surface; did not stage nested code, push, revert, call `update_goal`, edit root product code, or retry Hanwoo T-251.
- Reproduced with isolated installer state that an existing `settings.json` plus pre-created `settings.json.bak` directory made `python goal/scripts/install_goal.py <settings> goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` return exit `0`, print `Backup: <settings.json.bak>`, and modify `settings.json` even though no real backup file existed.
- Root cause: `backup_settings()` checked only `backup_path.exists()`, so any existing non-file path was accepted as an existing backup.
- Fixed `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` by rejecting an existing backup path unless it is a file, causing the installer to stop before writing settings.
- Added `tests/test_install_goal.py::test_install_cli_rejects_backup_directory_without_writing`.
- Direct after-fix QA in `.tmp/claude-goal-t1745b-installer-backup-directory-qa.json` confirms the backup-directory case returns exit `2`, leaves stdout empty, reports `goal installer error: backup path exists but is not a file`, keeps `settings.json` unchanged, leaves the backup directory intact, and prints no traceback.

## 2026-06-09 - Codex

- Completed T-1752 as a `claude-goal` direct artifact-option empty-value parity fix in `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, preserving existing nested WIP and the concurrently recorded T-1751 goal-record schema guard.
- Direct CLI interaction reproduced that `contract --verify-json=`, `contract --json-output=`, `contract --markdown-output=`, `contract --verify-schema=`, `doctor --install --verify-json=`, `doctor --install --json-output=`, `doctor --install --markdown-output=`, and `doctor --install --verify-markdown=` treated empty inline values as omitted and returned normal output or later dependency behavior instead of a value-required usage error.
- Equivalent slash `invoke` paths already rejected those empty values, so the defect was classified as 기능 오류 + 로딩/피드백 + 카피 caused by direct/slash parser policy drift.
- Official references checked: Python argparse value options and invalid-argument behavior, Click option value behavior, and GitHub CLI reference style; the adopted design was to make direct value options fail explicitly instead of silently ignoring user-supplied empty values.
- Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by validating direct `doctor` and `contract` namespace value options with `_validate_option_value()`, matching slash parser behavior.
- Added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_direct_artifact_options_reject_empty_inline_values_without_mutating`.
- A/B QA in `.tmp/claude-goal-t1752-direct-empty-artifact-options-qa.json` records baseline score `0/8`, candidate score `8/8`, decision `adopt_candidate`, and confirms all eight direct empty artifact-option cases return exit `2`, leave stdout empty, report `goal error: <option> requires a value`, print no traceback, and keep the active goal unchanged.

## 2026-06-09 - Codex

- Completed T-1754 as a `claude-goal` stale-days empty-value guard fix in `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, preserving existing nested WIP and the concurrently recorded T-1753 direct JSON empty session-id parity surface.
- Direct CLI reproduction showed `doctor --stale-days= --json`, `pause-stale --stale-days= --json`, and split-empty `--stale-days ""` variants reached inconsistent feedback: direct commands exposed argparse `invalid int value: ''`, while slash `invoke` paths classified the same empty value as `--stale-days must be an integer`.
- Confirmed the correct behavior should be a missing-value usage error before DB open, with empty stdout, no traceback, and no state mutation.
- Root cause: `_parse_stale_days_value()` called `int(value)` without first validating blank values, and direct `doctor`/`pause-stale` argparse definitions used `type=int`, so empty strings were consumed before the shared option validator.
- Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by routing direct stale-days values through `_parse_stale_days_value()`, validating blank values with `_validate_option_value("--stale-days", ...)`, and removing direct argparse `type=int` for stale-days options.
- Updated `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_invoke_stale_days_rejects_non_integer_before_db_open` to keep non-integer cases separate and added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_stale_days_rejects_empty_values_before_db_open` for direct/slash inline-empty and split-empty coverage.

## 2026-06-09 - Codex

- Completed T-1756 as a `claude-goal` quoted invoke objective token-option parse fix in `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, preserving existing nested WIP and the completed T-1755 direct value-option missing-value surface.
- Direct isolated CLI reproduction showed `invoke "objective text" --tokens 10`, `invoke "objective text" --tokens=10`, and quoted-objective invalid/missing token-budget variants failed before set fallback parsing with `goal error: No closing quotation`.
- Confirmed the same quoted objective without options worked, and unquoted word-by-word objective plus token options worked, so the defect was specific to multi-argv shell-quoted objective text followed by token options.
- Root cause: `handle_main_invoke()` round-trips multi-argv input through `shell_command(*args.args)`, which correctly shell-quotes an argv containing spaces, but `_parse_invoke_command()` split that shell-escaped string with plain `.split(maxsplit=1)`, breaking the opening quote before `parse_set_args()` could use `shlex.split()`.
- Fixed `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` by making `_parse_invoke_command()` parse command boundaries with `shlex.split()` and rebuild `rest` with `shell_command(*parts[1:])`.
- Added `tests/test_claude_goal.py::test_invoke_quoted_objective_preserves_token_options`.
- After-fix QA in `.tmp/claude-goal-t1756-quoted-invoke-token-qa.json` records score `4/4`, decision `adopt_candidate`, and confirms quoted-objective valid split/inline token-budget forms set the intended objective and budget, while invalid/missing values return the specific token-budget usage errors without `No closing quotation` or traceback.
- Verification passed focused regression pytest (`1 passed`), invoke/set cluster pytest (`28 passed, 109 deselected`), full isolated pytest with `PYTHONUTF8=1` (`196 passed`), Ruff check, scoped Ruff format check (`9 files already formatted`), py_compile, `git diff --check` with existing CRLF warnings only, and graph detect risk `0.00`.
- No nested code staging, push, revert, `update_goal`, root product edit, or Hanwoo T-251 retry was performed.

## 2026-06-09 - Codex

- Completed T-1782 as a `claude-goal` `install.sh` help/unknown-argument side-effect boundary fix in `claude-goal/` on branch `improve/goal-system`, preserving existing nested WIP plus the already-recorded T-1781 completion-audit objective preflight, T-1780 install output quoting, and T-1779 doctor strict install-required fixes.
- Direct isolated `install.sh` probes with repo-local temp `HOME` showed `./install.sh --help` and `./install.sh --dry-run` ignored their arguments, performed normal install, wrote `.claude/settings.json`, and created `.claude/skills/goal`.
- Classification: functional error, loading/feedback, and copy. External reference checked: GNU command-line standards say `--help` should print invocation help and not perform the normal command function.
- Root cause: `install.sh` had no argument parser, so every invocation flowed straight into side-effecting install work.
- Fixed `install.sh` by adding `usage()`, early `-h|--help` handling with exit `0`, and explicit unknown-option/unexpected-argument errors with exit `2` before any install directories/files are created.

- Reproduced a Stop-hook detector false negative where GNU `env` combined short-option forms `env -vu CLAUDE_GOAL_DB python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `env -vC DIR python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `/usr/bin/env -vS "python -u" claude_goal.py stop-hook`, and structured `["-vu", "CLAUDE_GOAL_DB", "python", ...]` were not counted as `/goal` hooks.
- Verified actual execution with Git for Windows `env.exe`: `-vu` unset the env var and executed Python, `-vC` changed directory and executed Python, and `-vS` split `python -u` and executed Python; all wrote the marker through the target script with `stop-hook`.
- Root cause: `env` wrapper detection only consumed separate `-v`, `-u`, `-C`, and `-S` tokens, not GNU combined short-option tokens.
- `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now consume reproduced `-v+[uC]` wrapper operands and route `-v+S` through the existing split-string launcher path, without treating `env -vu ... echo claude_goal.py stop-hook` as a goal hook.
- `tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `tests/test_install_goal.py` cover shell-form, structured, migration, and false-positive echo variants.
- Verification passed manual before/after detector probes (`False False` to `True True`; combined-option echo stayed `False False`), focused doctor/installer tests (`3 passed` and `1 passed`), full nested suite after formatting (`385 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check, `py_compile`, path-limited `git diff --check`, and nested graph detect risk `0.00`.
- Temporary `.tmp/pytest-t2085-*` directories were removed.
- Changed files for this slice: `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py`, `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py`, `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py`, `claude-goal/tests/test_install_goal.py`, `.ai/HANDOFF.md`, `.ai/TASKS.md`, and `.ai/SESSION_LOG.md`.

- Reproduced a Stop-hook detector false negative where `env -v python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, `/usr/bin/env --debug python claude_goal.py stop-hook`, and structured `["-v", "python", ...]` were not counted as `/goal` hooks.
- Verified actual execution with Git for Windows `env.exe`: `-v` and `--debug` returned `0`, emitted debug output, and wrote the marker through the Python script with `stop-hook`.
- Root cause: `env` wrapper detection did not consume no-operand debug options before resolving the Python launcher.
- `goal/scripts/claude_goal.py` and `goal/scripts/install_goal.py` now strip `-v`/`--debug` before launcher validation, without treating `env -v echo claude_goal.py stop-hook` as a goal hook.
- `tests/test_claude_goal.py` and `tests/test_install_goal.py` cover shell-form, structured, migration, and false-positive echo variants.
- Verification passed manual before/after detector probes (`False False` to `True True`; env/debug echo stayed `False False`), focused doctor/installer tests (`3 passed` and `1 passed`), full nested suite (`385 passed`), Ruff check, Ruff format check, `py_compile`, path-limited `git diff --check`, and nested graph detect risk `0.00`.
- Temporary `.tmp/pytest-t2083-*` directories were removed.
- Changed files for this slice: `claude-goal/goal/scripts/claude_goal.py`, `claude-goal/goal/scripts/install_goal.py`, `claude-goal/tests/test_claude_goal.py`, `claude-goal/tests/test_install_goal.py`, `.ai/HANDOFF.md`, `.ai/TASKS.md`, and `.ai/SESSION_LOG.md`.
